title: Agallochum grandiflorum
taxonomy: Agallochum grandiflorum is a plant species within the family Agalomemnaceae. However, some sources classify it as a genus within the family Lauraceae. The plant is native to Southeast Asia, but its classification status remains somewhat unclear.
uses: Agallochum grandiflorum is a plant species native to various parts of the world, and research has identified its potential medicinal uses. benefits: Agallochum grandiflorum, also known as Agarwood, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
